content: Everybody who was appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 will continue. By next September, either this Budget session or the Monsoon session, a new law is likely to be enacted, says Attorney General R Venkataramani adding that about 21 members are due to retire in the meantime.

title: CJI calls on High Court collegiums to consider female lawyers of Supreme Court for judgeship
content: The CJI requested the High Court collegiums to widen the zone of their consideration and include women advocates from their states who are practising in the Supreme Court for elevation.

title: HALEU-Thorium fuel unsuitable for Indian nuclear reactors: study
content: The proponents of ANEEL, a new type of nuclear fuel, have suggested it can be dropped into existing reactors, including PHWRs, allowing them to use thorium today

title: The quiet demographic revolution unfolding in India
content: India, long viewed as the archetype of a ‘high-fertility developing country’, has quietly become a relatively low-fertility society

title: Khamenei killing: Mehbooba Mufti, NC MPs from J&K visit Iran Embassy in Delhi to express condolences
content: “We pray that Iran emerges as victorious in its fight against evil, as it stands alone. It seems all those whose names surfaced in the Epstein files are standing with Israel,” the PDP chief said

title: PM must take responsibility for foreign policy failure and resign: J&K Congress chief
content: Congress workers held street protests in parts of J&K against the proposed India-U.S. trade deal and alleged it was bound to “affect local economic interests”; several leaders detained

title: West Bengal SIR test: reading the Supreme Court’s order
content: Why did the Supreme Court order the deployment of judicial officers to decide electoral roll disputes in West Bengal? What triggered the debate of whether the Election Commission should be equipped with permanent staff? With 60 lakh cases pending adjudication, what does publication of final rolls mean?

title: 'Willing to talk to Iran', says Trump, but warns new leader won't 'live in peace'
content: Trump said that the Iran war was going to "end soon", without providing any specific timeline. He also warned Tehran that if they tried "anything cute", the US would end the nation. He called the Iran war a "short term excursion." During a CBS interview, he said that the war was "very complete" and warned Iran against doing "anything cute".

title: 'Don't get eliminated yourself': Iran warns Trump, slaps down 'empty' threats
content: Iran's security official warned President Trump to be careful after the US leader threatened harsher retaliation for any Strait of Hormuz disruption.  Tehran continues regional attacks, with missile and drone strikes hitting Dubai, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait. Iran's parliament speaker indicated no ceasefire is sought, emphasizing retaliation against aggressors.  Global oil prices have surged due to the conflict.

title: Nvidia plans AI agent platform 'NemoClaw', will not make OpenAl’s million-dollar mistake
content: Nvidia is reportedly launching NemoClaw, an open-source AI agent platform for enterprises, prioritizing security from the outset. This move addresses the security concerns that plagued the viral OpenClaw project.  Nvidia aims to set industry standards and partner with major tech firms, offering a secure way to deploy AI agents for employee tasks.

title: One terrorist killed, operation under way along LoC in J&K's Nowshera
content: Indian Army troops thwarted an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control in Jammu and Kashmir's Jhangar sector on Tuesday. Acting on intelligence, alert soldiers engaged two terrorists, successfully eliminating one Pakistan-sponsored infiltrator and preventing any breach of the LoC. A search operation is underway for the second terrorist.

title: India slides down in 2026 GECI rankings: How can it be improved?
content: India slipped to 25th in the 2026 Gender Equality in Chess Index (GECI), highlighting gender imbalance in the sport. Despite strong performances by elite women players, female participation in youth championships remains low, and many girls drop out early. Experts say social pressures, financial hurdles and lack of structured support limit opportunities, stressing the need for better grassroots development and long-term systems to promote women’s chess.

title: Anthropic takes US govt to court; CFO says: After President Trump’s social media posts, a major ...
content: Anthropic CEO apologized for a memo after talks with the Pentagon broke down, only to file a lawsuit against the Trump administration days later. The company seeks to overturn a national security threat designation, claiming it jeopardizes billions in revenue and irreparably harms its business and investor confidence.

title: 'Dream is to play senior World Cup': Yash Dhull opens up on heart surgery and comeback
content: Under-19 World Cup-winning captain Yash Dhull faced a challenging period after heart surgery, battling a strong desire to return to cricket.  Despite initial setbacks and a risky recovery, Dhull's focus remains on regaining fitness and aiming for a senior World Cup appearance, drawing inspiration from senior players and preparing for future opportunities.

title: Global fuel crisis looms: Gulf nations cut oil output; Hormuz disruptions choke supply
content: The Strait of Hormuz closure is forcing Gulf nations to slash oil production as storage tanks fill. Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, and Iraq have significantly reduced output, with Iraq facing an imminent shutdown. Alternative routes are insufficient, raising fears of a total production halt and impacting global oil prices.

title: Amazon sends complaint letter to FCC against Elon Musk's Starlink
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and space safety details. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, would take centuries to deploy, and could monopolize orbital resources, hindering future space endeavors.

title: How much has US-Iran war hit India's oil, LPG and LNG supply? Top 10 points to know
content: India, which is the world’s third-largest importer and consumer of oil, maintains underground strategic petroleum reserves with a capacity of 5.33 million tonnes. However, these reserves are currently filled to about 80 per cent.

title: Ahead of Tamil Nadu polls, Cabinet approves international airport status for Madurai
content: The Union Cabinet has approved declaring Madurai airport an international airport, a move aimed at boosting global connectivity to the temple city. This decision is expected to enhance air connectivity, strengthen Madurai's role as an educational and healthcare hub, and attract international pilgrims and businesses, supporting regional trade and economic activity.

title: Chhattisgarh Tribal woman lynched over witchcraft suspicion; husband, 3 others held
content: A tribal woman in Chhattisgarh's Jashpur district was allegedly killed in a superstition-linked attack after being accused of witchcraft. Her husband, co-wife, and two others have been arrested for the brutal assault, which police believe was motivated by accusations of practising sorcery and causing family misfortunes.

title: Arshdeep Singh penalised by ICC following India's T20 World Cup triumph
content: Indian pacer Arshdeep Singh has been fined 15% of his match fee and received one demerit point for a Level 1 breach of the ICC Code of Conduct. The incident occurred during the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand when he aggressively threw the ball, hitting batter Daryl Mitchell. Arshdeep accepted the sanction, avoiding a formal hearing.

title: Iran war impact on India: LPG shortage concerns rise; which states may feel the heat?
content: West Asia tensions are disrupting commercial LPG supplies to eateries in Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Chennai. Authorities are prioritizing domestic use, impacting businesses. The government is ramping up LPG production, extending refill cycles, and forming a committee to address commercial supply requests amid import concerns.

title: Iran war: Oil surge, midterms worry Trump advisers as they push for an exit plan
content: Amidst surging oil prices and upcoming mid-term elections, President Trump has reportedly been advised to seek an exit plan from the Middle East. Advisors are urging him to articulate that military objectives have largely been achieved, as polls indicate public opposition to the ongoing conflict.

title: Middle East crisis: Asian airlines raise fares as war fuels oil shock fears
content: Asian airlines are hiking fares and planning for potential flight groundings as the Middle East conflict fuels a major oil shock. Indian carriers have already raised long-haul ticket prices by 15%, with Vietnam warning of up to 70% increases. Many Asian airlines, lacking robust fuel hedging, face significant vulnerability as jet fuel prices double, threatening operations and travel demand.

title: 'Ispe mein kya bolun?': Kishan gets annoyed after winning T20 WC title
content: India’s 2026 T20 World Cup win sparked controversy after Kirti Azad criticised the team for taking the trophy to a Hindu temple. Ishan Kishan brushed off questions about the remark, urging reporters to focus on the victory. Kishan also reflected on his comeback journey, while India sealed the title with a dominant win over New Zealand.

title: Uttam Nagar murder case: Accused’s mother moves Delhi high court fearing house demolition
content: The mother of an accused in the Uttam Nagar murder case has approached the Delhi High Court, fearing her house might be demolished like that of a co-accused. This action follows a demolition drive by civic authorities in connection with a Holi clash that resulted in a death.

title: ‘ENHYPEN's Heeseung leaves group: ‘A clear musical path he wishes to pursue’
content: K-pop sensation ENHYPEN's Heeseung is embarking on a solo career, departing from group activities in March 2026. The agency confirmed the decision stems from Heeseung's distinct musical aspirations, with the remaining six members continuing as ENHYPEN. Heeseung expressed gratitude to his bandmates and fans, promising a new album soon.

title: Sikandar Co-Star defends Salman Khan after AR Murugadoss blames him for failure
content: Actor Jatin Sarna defends Salman Khan's punctuality on the 'Sikandar' set, refuting director AR Murugadoss's claims of the star's alleged lack of commitment. Sarna highlighted Salman's hard work and support, even while dealing with injuries. Murugadoss, however, maintains his stance on the film's underperformance.

title: Pune Porsche crash case: SC grants bail to accused driver’s father
content: The Supreme Court granted bail to the father of the minor accused in the 2024 Pune Porsche crash, who allegedly manipulated blood samples. He had been in custody for 22 months. The accident, which killed two software engineers, occurred when the inebriated juvenile allegedly crashed into their motorcycle.

title: Pune model alleges harassment after inter-faith marriage, reconverts to Hinduism
content: A Pimpri-Chinchwad model has reconverted to Hinduism after alleging mental and physical harassment during her marriage to a Muslim businessman. She claims she was pressured to abandon Hindu practices and felt influenced into the marriage, which she now calls 'love jihad'. Enduring years of abuse for her children, she has filed a police complaint and seeks security.

title: 'SKY is the limit': Celebrations galore at Suryakumar Yadav's UP village
content: India's T20 World Cup 2026 victory sparked jubilant celebrations in Suryakumar Yadav's ancestral village, Hathauda. Relatives and villagers cheered the win, which continued into Monday with dhols and greetings for his grandfather. The family had offered prayers for the team's success, with his mother visiting Kashi Vishwanath Temple.

title: Strait of Hormuz crisis: Saudi's Aramco CEO warns of 'catastrophic consequences' for oil markets
content: Saudi Aramco warns that ongoing conflict in the Strait of Hormuz could lead to catastrophic consequences for global oil markets, citing already low inventories and potential impacts on various industries. The company confirmed a small fire at its Ras Tanura Refinery following an attack, which has since been brought under control.

title: ‘He always plays with intent’: Ashwin hails Samson but rues his missed T20 WC hundreds
content: Ravichandran Ashwin shared his thoughts on Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup 2026 performances. Ashwin stated Samson narrowly missed three centuries, scoring 97 not out against West Indies, 89 against England, and 89 against New Zealand. He highlighted Samson's intent to play aggressively, even at the cost of personal records. Samson's batting was crucial for India's title win.

title: Energy crisis hits Pak: Sharif govt takes drastic steps amid Middle East tensions
content: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has introduced austerity measures to combat fuel shortages caused by the Middle East crisis. These include cutting government spending, reducing fuel allowances for official vehicles, and implementing a work-from-home policy for public sector employees. The government aims to save fuel and mitigate the impact of rising oil import bills.

title: Fuel crisis in Bangladesh: India to supply 5,000 tons of diesel to Dhaka
content: India began supplying 5,000 tons of diesel to Bangladesh via pipeline on Tuesday, part of an annual agreement for 180,000 tons. This delivery aims to alleviate fuel shortages and combat illegal hoarding, as the Bangladesh government intensifies efforts to monitor stock levels and enforce supply limits.

title: BCCI to honour five ICC title-winning Indian teams at awards ceremony
content: The BCCI will host its annual awards ceremony, NAMAN, in New Delhi on March 15 to felicitate multiple Indian teams for their ICC trophy wins over the past year. Players, coaches, and support staff from the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, ICC Champions Trophy 2025, senior women's team, U19 boys, and U19 girls' victorious squads will be honoured.

title: Jamie Lever opens up about brother Jesse Lever’s tumour battle
content: In a gripping revelation, Jamie Lever recounted the tumultuous journey her family endured following her brother Jesse's childhood diagnosis of a perilous neck tumour. After navigating a perilous surgery that didn’t go as planned, they pursued groundbreaking treatments in the United States. Jesse's remarkable recovery transformed their lives, forging an unbreakable bond and deepening their faith in overcoming adversity.

title: Parliament face-off: Centre, oppn clash as debate on no-confidence motion against Birla begins
content: Lok Sabha witnessed a heated debate as the opposition moved a resolution to remove Speaker Om Birla. Congress MPs questioned the authority of Jagdambika Pal, who was presiding, citing a constitutional vacuum due to the absence of a Deputy Speaker. The government defended Pal's position, leading to sharp exchanges.

title: 'Kar diya na kaam': BJP shares video of Rahul Gandhi praising IYC's 'shirtless' protest
content: The BJP has criticized Rahul Gandhi for backing the Youth Congress' "shirtless" protest at the AI Impact Summit in Delhi. Party leaders accused Gandhi of orchestrating the disruption, which they claim embarrassed India globally. Meanwhile, a court reserved its order on the bail pleas of three Youth Congress workers involved in the protest.

title: Interfaith wedding drama in Thane: Nikah halted after girl alleges rape, conversion bid by tailor
content: A 20-year-old Bhiwandi tailor was arrested for allegedly raping a minor he befriended on social media two years ago. He reportedly attempted to force her into an inter-faith marriage upon her turning 18. Hindutva activists intervened at the planned 'nikah' on March 6, leading to the girl's rescue and the accused's booking under Pocso and IPC sections.

title: ‘Kash aap mere paas hote’: Rinku’s emotional tribute to father after T20 WC win
content: Indian batter Rinku Singh dedicated the T20 World Cup triumph to his late father, Khanchand Singh, who passed away during the tournament.  Rinku expressed profound grief, sharing how his father's dream of seeing him as a world champion was fulfilled, but lamented his absence.  He spoke of his father's teachings on duty and the immense void left behind.

title: Is Iran war draining US weapons stockpiles? Billions spent in first 48hrs of operation
content: The US reportedly spent $5.6 billion on weaponry in the first 48 hours of operations against Iran, raising concerns about depleting advanced military supplies. US Central Command detailed over 5,000 targets struck, including naval assets and military infrastructure. President Trump issued a stern warning to Iran against disrupting oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, vowing severe retaliation.

title: BCCI announces Rs 131 crore reward for India after T20 World Cup 2026 triumph
content: Team India has been awarded a Rs 131 crore cash reward by the BCCI following their historic ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 victory. Dominating New Zealand by 96 runs in the final, India successfully defended their title, becoming the first team to win the T20 World Cup three times and solidifying their status as a premier cricketing nation.

title: Kritika Kamra and Gaurav Kapur’s wedding after-party invite details leaked
content: Actress Kritika Kamra and host Gaurav Kapur are set for an intimate Mumbai wedding on March 11-12, 2026.  They've chosen a modern, minimal celebration over a grand destination event. An after-party invitation hints at a relaxed, "unhurried" evening in South Mumbai, emphasizing authenticity for their new chapter. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'Like they robbed a bank in broad daylight': CFB's unique tampering etiquette
content: coach Dabo Swinney calls out Ole Miss for "blatant" tampering . he says school tried to lure a Clemson player into the transfer portal . coach says he bailed for more money at Ole miss .

title: Why did the Chiefs sign Kenneth Walker III, and what's next for Kansas City?
content: the Seahawks went 6-11 in 2025, their worst record since 2012 . he has split carries in the league, landing between 153 and 221 . his contract is set to become one of the largest deals for a running back in league history .

title: Dolphins signing Malik Willis to replace Tua: Answering 6 key questions
content: the Miami Dolphins agree to a three-year, $67.5 million deal with quarterback malik Willis . the deal presumably makes him the team's starting quarterback for at least the 2026 season . first-year general manager Jon-Eric Sullivan played coy with his interest in him at the NFL combine .

title: Panthers land Phillips on 4-year deal, sources say
content: former Dolphins linebacker jaelan Phillips agrees to a four-year, $120 million deal with the Carolina Panthers . the deal includes $80 million in guarantees, according to ESPN NFL Insider Adam Schefter . Phillips called landing in Philadelphia "literally the greatest thing that has happened to me"

title: Is the Super Bowl MVP an RB1 in Fantasy? Kenneth Walker III moves to Kansas City
content: the Kansas City Chiefs plan to sign super bowl LX MVP Kenneth Walker III during free agency . he is coming off the second 1,000-yard rushing season of his four-year career . the Chiefs have long been viewed as a top-10 fantasy option at the position .
